name: Sopdet
description: Sopdet, also known as Sothis in Greek, is an ancient Egyptian goddess personifying the star Sirius, the brightest star in the night sky. Her appearance heralded the annual flooding of the Nile, a crucial event for Egyptian agriculture and prosperity. Sopdet was revered as a bringer of life and renewal, symbolizing fertility and abundance. She is closely associated with the new year and the cyclical nature of time. Often depicted as a woman wearing a headdress featuring a five-pointed star or a white crown with horns, Sopdet is sometimes shown riding a cow, further linking her to fertility and nourishment. She is considered the consort of Sah, the constellation Orion, and the mother of Sopdu, a falcon-headed god guarding the eastern border of Egypt. Sopdet's importance stemmed from her connection to the Nile's inundation, making her a vital figure in ensuring the well-being of the Egyptian people. Her worship dates back to the Old Kingdom, and she remained a significant deity throughout Egyptian history. Temples dedicated to her were often aligned with the rising of Sirius, emphasizing her celestial significance. She embodies hope, renewal, and the life-giving power of the Nile.
subtitle: Goddess of Sirius, herald of the Nile

keyMyths.title: The Rising of Sothis
keyMyths.description: The heliacal rising of Sopdet (Sirius) coincided with the annual flooding of the Nile, marking the start of the Egyptian new year and a time of great celebration and anticipation for a bountiful harvest.
keyMyths.source: Various Egyptian texts and archaeological evidence

keyMyths.title: Consort of Sah
keyMyths.description: Sopdet is regarded as the consort of Sah, the personification of the constellation Orion. Together, they represent a celestial family, with Sopdu as their son, guarding Egypt's eastern frontier.

keyMyths.title: Sopdet and the Afterlife
keyMyths.description: Sopdet's association with rebirth and renewal extended to beliefs about the afterlife. She was seen as a guide and protector, ensuring the deceased's successful transition to the next world.

sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
sources.work: The Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of Egyptian deities, including Sopdet.
sources.text: A comprehensive overview of Egyptian deities, including Sopdet.
